fields.title: agent: LRU eviction on spawn when at capacity
fields.description: When spawn hits maxThreads, instead of failing with AgentLimitReached immediately, try to evict the least recently used agent first. This is separate from GC (which cleans up dead agents). LRU answers: when the orchestrator needs a new agent but the pool is full, which existing agent should be closed to make room?nnCurrent behavior: spawn checks HashMap.size(agents) >= maxThreads and fails with AgentLimitReached.nnDesired behavior: spawn checks the cap, and if full, finds the least recently touched agent (by lastActivityAt), cascading-closes it (reusing existing shutdown + collectDescendants), then proceeds with the spawn. Only fail with AgentLimitReached if every agent is still actively running (no evictable candidate).nnAn agent is evictable if it is in a final state (completed/failed/shutdown) and has no active children. Among evictable candidates, pick the one with the oldest lastActivityAt.
fields.design: 1. Add lastActivityRef: Ref<HashMap<AgentId, number>> in manager. Update it on spawn, on prompt (send), and on wait completion — any interaction counts as 'use'.nn2. Add evictLru helper inside AgentManagerLive (not on the public interface — internal to spawn). It: (a) collects all agents in final state without active children, (b) sorts by lastActivityAt ascending, (c) calls shutdown on the oldest (cascading), (d) returns true if something was evicted, false if nothing evictable.nn3. In spawn, replace the hard AgentLimitReached check with: if at cap → call evictLru → if eviction succeeded, continue spawn → if nothing evictable, fail with AgentLimitReached.nn4. Reuse existing createdAtRef as lastActivityRef (rename it). Update its timestamp on: spawn completion, send (prompt), wait completion. This tracks recency of use, not just creation.nn5. No new public API surface. No new error types. The only observable change is that spawn succeeds where it previously failed, by transparently closing a stale agent.
fields.acceptance_criteria: - Spawning when at maxThreads with at least one completed agent succeeds (evicts the LRU completed agent).n- Spawning when at maxThreads with all agents actively running fails with AgentLimitReached.n- Eviction uses cascading teardown (children are closed too).n- lastActivityAt is updated on spawn, send, and wait — not just creation.n- npm run gate passes.
fields.notes: Final validation rerun after touch implementation adjustment: npm run gate passes in extensions/tau (typecheck/lint/test), same pre-existing lint warnings only.
fields.close_reason: Implemented: spawn auto-evicts LRU completed agent at capacity. touch() tracks recency on send/wait.
